_meta.description: Customs Border Control Area rules. Goods with FOB > USD 1,000 require CBCA clearance unless exempted. Some goods always require CBCA regardless of value (cbca_always=true).

always_required_reasons.22: Alcoholic beverages — always CBCA regardless of value (excise goods)
always_required_reasons.24: Tobacco products — always CBCA regardless of value (excise goods)
always_required_reasons.27: Petroleum products — always CBCA (strategic goods / excise)
always_required_reasons.87: Motor vehicles — always CBCA (high-value regulated goods)
always_required_reasons.93: Arms and ammunition — always CBCA (security clearance required)

exemptions.condition: Importer is an accredited diplomatic mission
Supported by Ministry of Finance exemption letter

exemptions.description: Personal effects on change of residence
exemptions.condition: Importer returning resident with proof of domicile change

exemptions.description: Trade samples with no commercial value
exemptions.condition: FOB value below USD 50 and marked as samples

cbca_goods_categories: Textiles, clothing and footwear (when FOB > USD 1,000)

cbca_process.step1: Pre-arrival notification to ZIMRA within 48 hours of vessel departure
cbca_process.step2: Submit cargo manifest to CBCA office
cbca_process.step3: Goods scanned/inspected at designated CBCA post
cbca_process.step5: Goods released to bonded warehouse pending full duty payment

penalties.goods_seizure: Non-compliant goods subject to seizure and forfeiture
penalties.agent_liability: Clearing agent jointly liable for CBCA non-compliance
